titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/66

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=608 status=draft modified=2017-01-30 19:28:39.043988+00 | translation moa=609 status=draft modified=2023-03-27 14:56:23.837372+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris Walter filius Alani dapifer Regis Scott’ salutem . Sciant tam presentes quam futuri me dedisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie
de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . pro animabus dauid et Melcolmi Regum dominorum meorum et Comitis henrici et patris mei et Matris mee et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . et pro salute Willelmi Regis Scott’
et dauid fratris eius . et pro salute mea et omnium heredum meorum . in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam totam terram de Makelin per diuisas quas Duuenald et Gillebride meo precepto perambulauerunt inter terram de
makelin et terram Gilberti filii Richeri . scilicet ab illo loco quo Mulneburne cadit in ar’ subtus culteicouan et ascendendo per eandem burnam usque ad fulelech . et sic ascendendo per eundem sicum ex transuerso mus
so . usque ad burnam que descendit de rederburne . et ascendendo per burnam ex transuerso condoso de rederburne usque in burnam que cadit in duueloch . et per burnam que descendit de duueloch descendendo per eandem burnam
usque in saxnoc et ascendendo per saxnoc usque ad polmulin et sic ascendendo per polmulin usque per medium acthenebron . et inde sursum usque ad condosum et sic per condosum sicut aqua defluunt uersus eos
ex australi parte ad caput Ned . et sic deorsum per ned usque in ar’ . et per ar’ deorsum usque lugar’ . et per lugar’ sursum usque ad dufpol . et per Dufpol sursum per diuisas quas feci perambulare eis
usque econtra monecapel . Cum hac terra dedi eis landas et halghes ex aquilonali parte fluminis ar’ quas uoluerint excolere et piscaturam in ostio fluminis ar’ cum uno rete pri
mo scilicet de tribus retibus . et desuper ut utantur ipso flumine ar’ tam in piscationibus quam in aliis rebus sicut eis placuere et utilius fuerit . et sicut ego ipse ad meum proprium opus melius habui . Hanc elemo
sinam . uolo ut predicti Monachi de Melros . ita libere et quiete et honorifice perpetuo tenore ab omnibus seruitiis et placitis asisis quoque et auxiliis et omni exactione seculari . et consuetudine in perpetu
um [mis-written perpetutim] teneant et possideant . sicut liberius et quietius et honorificentius aliqua elemosina teneri potest et possideri . Preterea . dedi et concessi eis totam pasturam foreste mee usque ad diuisas de Doueglas
et de lesmagu et de Glengeuel a parte . aquilonali fluminis ar’ cum omnibus asiamentis . in nemore et in omnibus eis necessariis et unam carrucatam terre ad excolendam in illis locis ubi prouiderint
melius et utilius esse ad opus suum libere et quiete de me et heredibus meis ab omnibus seruitiis perpetuo tenore possidendam excepto quod quinque marcas argenti mihi et heredibus meis annuatim persoluent . salua
tamen foresta mea tantum in bestiis et auibus . et hoc sciendum quod ego et heredes mei faciemus forense seruitium apud dominum nostrum regem de omnibus predictis et warantizabimus eis predictas diui
sas et quicquid in eis est in bosco et in plano et in omnibus aliis rebus contra omnes homines in perpetuum . sicut liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam . et quia uolui ut hec mea elemosina firma esset
et stabilis nec aliquo modo posset infringi dominus meus Willelmus rex prece mea omnia predicta carta sua confirmauit . sicut suam propriam elemosinam . Teste alano filio meo . liulfo filio Ma’
Roberto de Constetin . Roberto de Monte Gumeri . Roberto filio fulberti . Roberto filio Maias . Radulfo de kent . Walter Constentin . Ricardo clerico . Ricardo Wallensi . Ada de Neutuna .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, as well present as future, Walter son of Alan, steward of the king of the Scots, sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, for the sake of the souls of my lords, David and Malcolm the kings, and of Earl Henry, and of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors and successors, and for the salvation of William king of Scots and David his brother, and for the salvation of my own soul and of my heirs' souls, in free and pure and everlasting alms, the whole land of Mauchline by the boundaries which Donald and Gilla Brigte at my instruction perambulated between the land of Mauchline and the land of Gilbert son of Richer, to wit, from the place where 'Mulneburne' falls into the Ayr below 'Culteicouan', and going up by the same burn as far as 'Fulelech', and so going upwards by the same syke, and across the bog as far as the burn which descends from 'Rederburne' to the burn which falls into 'Duueloch', and by the burn which comes down from 'Duueloch', descending by the same burn into 'Saxnoc', and going up by 'Saxnoc' to 'Polmulin', and thus ascending by 'Polmulin' up to the midst of 'Acthenebron', and from there upwards as far as the ridge and so by the ridge as the water flows across them from the south side to the head of the Nith, and thus downwards by the Nith as far as the Ayr, and by the Ayr downwards to the Lugar, and by the Lugar upwards to the 'Dufpol', and by the 'Dufpol' upwards by the boundaries which I caused them to perambulate, as far as the other side of 'Monecapel'. With this land I have given to them the open lands and haughs on the north side of the river Ayr which they might want to cultivate, and a fishery at the mouth of the river Ayr, with one net at first, to wit, of the three nets; and in addition that they may use the river Ayr as well for fishing as for other things as might please and benefit them; and just as I myself have well had this alms for my own use, I will that the aforesaid monks of Melrose as freely, quitly and honourably should hold and possess as a perpetual holding from all services and pleas of assize also and aids and every secular exaction and custom for ever, just as freely and quitly and honourably as any alms can be held and possessed. In addition, I have given and granted to them the whole pasture of my forest up to the boundaries of Douglas and Lesmahagow and Glengavel on the north side of the river Ayr, with all easements, in wood and in all things necessary for them, and one ploughgate of land for cultivating in those places where

And be it known that I and my heirs shall do forinsec service for our lord the king for all the aforesaid, and we shall warrant to them the aforesaid boundaries and whatever is in them, in wood and in plain, and in all other things, against all men for ever, as free and pure and perpetual alms. And because I have willed that this my alms should be strong and established nor by any means infringed, my lord King William has at my request confirmed all the things aforesaid by his charter, just as his own alms. Witness Alan my son, Liulf son of Master Robert of Côtentin, Robert of Montgomery, Robert son of Fulbert, Robert son of Maias, Ralph of Kent, Walter Côtentin, Richard the clerk, Richard Wallce, Adam of Newton.
